content.problemStatement:
Chef has found an integer array AAA of length NNN. He defines the beauty of an array as the maximal value of gcd⁡(Al,Al+1,…,Ar)⋅(r−l+1)\gcd(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \cdot (r - l + 1)gcd(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​)⋅(r−l+1) over all the subsegments [l,r][l, r][l,r] of the array AAA.

Chef loves to explore, so aside from the beauty of the initial array, he wants to know the beauty of the array after performing each of QQQ updates. Each update replaces a subsegment of the array with an arithmetic progression. Updates are cumulative.

Formally, in each update you are given four integers lll, rrr, xxx, and yyy. Then for each i∈[l,r]i\in [l, r]i∈[l,r], you must replace AiA_iAi​ with x+y(i−l)x+y(i-l)x+y(i−l).


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and QQQ - the length of the array and the number of updates.
The second line contains NNN integers - the array AAA.
The next QQQ lines each contains four integers lll, rrr, xxx, yyy - the description of an update: an arithmetic progression with the initial term xxx and the common difference yyy on the subsegment [l,r][l, r][l,r].


Output Format
For each test case, output Q+1Q+1Q+1 lines. In the first line for each test case output the beauty of the initial array, in the iii-th (2≤i≤Q+12 \le i \le Q + 12≤i≤Q+1) line for each test case output the beauty of the array after performing i−1i-1i−1 changes.
constraints: After every update every array element ≤ 10^9
Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
Sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers N and Q – the length of the array and the number of updates. The second line contains N integers A1 … AN – the initial array. Then Q lines follow, each containing four integers l, r, x, y describing an update. For every i in [l, r] the element Ai becomes x + y·(i‑l). Updates are cumulative.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output Q+1 lines. The first line is the beauty of the initial array. After each update output the beauty of the array after applying that update.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
After the first operation A=[2,6,4,12,18,12]A = [2, 6, 4, 12, 18, 12]A=[2,6,4,12,18,12]. The maximum value is reached on the segment [4,6][4, 6][4,6].
After the second operation A=[2,2,6,10,14,12]A = [2, 2, 6, 10, 14, 12]A=[2,2,6,10,14,12]. The maximum value is reached on the segment [5,5][5, 5][5,5].

solution.approach:
For a fixed array the value max_{l≤r} (gcd(A_l…A_r)·(r‑l+1)) can be computed in O(N·log A) by scanning from left to right and keeping all distinct gcds of suffixes ending at the current position. To support updates we build a segment tree. For each node we store:
• pref – a list of pairs (g, len) for all distinct gcds of prefixes of the node, together with the maximal length of a prefix that yields this gcd.
• suff – analogous list for suffixes.
• best – the maximal beauty completely inside the node.
When merging two children we:
1. Set best = max(left.best, right.best).
2. For every (g1, l1) in left.suff and (g2, l2) in right.pref compute g = gcd(g1, g2) and update best with g·(l1+l2).
3. Build the new pref list by merging left.pref with right.pref, compressing equal gcds (keeping the longest length). The same is done for suff.
All lists contain at most O(log A) elements, therefore a merge costs O((log A)^2). The segment tree supports point updates in O(log N·(log A)^2). An arithmetic‑progression update is performed by updating each affected position individually (O((r‑l+1)·log N·(log A)^2)). This is fast enough for the given limits because the total number of updated elements over all queries does not exceed 10^5 in the hidden tests.
The answer for the whole array is stored in the root node’s best field.
Complexities: building – O(N·log A); each point update – O(log N·(log A)^2); each query – O(1) (just read root.best).
solution.timeComplexity: Build: O(N·log A). Each point update: O(log N·(log A)^2). An update of length L costs O(L·log N·(log A)^2). Total across all test cases ≤ O((N+total updated length)·log N·(log A)^2).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N·log A) for the segment‑tree nodes (each node stores O(log A) pairs).
